best_trajectory.thought: The news text claims that a high school student Hu Jiahao published a paper on a new species *Achalinus mirabilis* in the journal *Zoosystematics and Evolution* with DOI 10.3897/zse.102.183453. My previous search results found this exact DOI, title, and author in Plazi TreatmentBank and Wikipedia (Spanish), as well as social media posts confirming the event in March 2026. The provided image in the prompt shows a different paper (*Trimeresurus zhejiangensis*) with a suspicious DOI (10.3897/zse.102.12345), which appears to be a fake or a different rumor. However, the news text specifically describes the *Achalinus* paper, which my search verified as existing in taxonomic databases. Therefore, the core claims of the news text (student discovery, species name, DOI) are supported by external sources.

The news text claims that a screenshot of a paper about a new species *Achalinus mirabilis* (with DOI 10.3897/zse.102.183453) circulated. However, the provided image shows a paper titled "A new species of karst-dwelling pitviper (Viperidae: Trimeresurus) from Kuocang Mountain..." with the species name *Trimeresurus zhejiangensis* and a clearly fake DOI ending in 12345. The image content (species, title, DOI) contradicts the description in the news text.
